epigraph.quote: The fire moves. The Griot moves with it. Not because there is always an audience. Because the truth does not wait.

acts.title: The Career Is What Happens Between

blocks.text: That is the most dangerous word in an actor's vocabulary.
The career is not the productions. The productions are the visible part. The career — the actual career — is what happens between them.
How the actor moves through the time when no camera is rolling. When no director has called. When the industry has not yet pointed in their direction.
The people who watched the daily video across the full length of this curriculum — who were present for Phase One's fire and Phase Two's silence and Phase Three's text and Phase Four's industry —
They were witnesses. In the circle. Around the fire. Not at the performance.
That community is not an audience for your performances.
It is your village. And the Griot who knows their village never works alone.
A blank script cover on the desk. He slides it across to her.
There is a truth you carry. Not the character's truth. Yours.
The work does not require permission. The work requires commitment. And the prior contract already contains that commitment.
The Griot did not ask the chief for permission.
the final words of the curriculum
She takes the blank pages. Holds them.
You have been somewhere. Let me tell you what you have done.
You went to the marrow. You built a person. You earned the voice. You entered the scene. You understood the industry. You kept the oath. You built the village.
Now — the fire is yours. Stand up. And begin.

requirement.prompt: Post your truth. The one from the blank pages. The one you came to acting to carry. Not the polished version. The actual one. Public. Witnessed. The Griot standing up. Include #JoelhoodP4L6.
